ety: S. मूलं
def: s.m. Root (of a plant or tree); a root (lit. and met.); basis, groundwork, beginning, principle, origin, source, cause; — bottom (of anything); foot of a mountain, &c.); lower part, or end; basis, foundation; — end, or juncture (of anything); — generation, race, stock; — original property, capital, principal, stock-in-trade (syn. aṣl); — original, original text (of any work, as distinguished from the ṭīkā, or commentary); — (in Arith.) the square root (i.q. barg-mūl; syn. jaẕr); — the twenty-fourth (or, according to some, the seventeenth, or the nineteenth) Nakshatra or lunar asterism (containing eleven stars, which appear to be the same as those in the tail of Scorpio, and are considered unlucky); — adj. & adv. Radical; original; proper; real; — radically; really
subs: s.m. The basis or text of a lesson, or lecture, or discourse
s.m. Original deed, document, or writing
or mūl-dhan, s.m. Original property; capital, principal, stock (syn. aṣluʼl-māl)
s.m. The essence, or drift, of wisdom
s.f. Equality with, or resemblance to, the root or origin
s.m. Connexion with the root, origin, or source
s.m. (in Gram.) A primary or simple vowel
s.m. The author of an original work; — an epithet of the Great creator
s.m. The original, or old-established, village; — the native village of the founder of a race or family
adj. (f. -vatī), Possessing roots; possessing edible roots; — practising magic with roots (?).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀽𑀮 (mūla), from Sanskrit मूल (mūla). Cognate with Bengali মূল (mul), Gujarati મૂળ (mūḷ).

ety: for A. منتفٍ, act. part. of اِنْتفي 'to be repulsed; to be removed,' &c., viii of نفي 'to remove,' &c.
def: part. adj. Repulsed; rejected; removed; dissipated, destroyed; — separated, retired (to one side).

ety: act. part. of اِنْطفأ, vii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Extinguished, quenched; extinct.

ety: pass. part. of اِنتہي; see muntahī
def: part. Finished, terminated; ending; — s.m. End, conclusion, term, boundary, extremity, utmost extent, height, summit (of).

ety: for A. منتهٍ muntahiṉ, act. part. of اِنْتہي 'to be finished,' &c., viii of نہي 'to arrive, to reach,' &c.
def: part. adj. Finished, terminated, ended, limited; ceased, stopped; who has finished his studies, learned, proficient

ety: pass. part. of انتج 'to bring forth, produce,' &c., iv of نتج 'to help (cattle
def: part. adj. Brought forth; — drawn out from; deduced, inferred, concluded; — s.m. That which is to be concluded, or inferred; conclusion, inference; consequence.

ety: pass. part. of اِنْتقل 'to be transported,' &c., viii of نقل 'to transport,' &c.
def: part. adj. Transported; carried; moved; transferred; — muntaqil (act. part. of the same verb), adj. Movable; transferable; migratory
subs: s.m. A transferee; an alienee; an assignee
v.t. To transfer; to alienate; to assign; to part with, dispose of, get rid of; — to endorse

ety: pass. part. of اِنْتقش 'to order (the stone of a ring, &c.
def: part. adj. Engraved; carved; — enchased; — painted.

ety: pass. part. of اِنتخب 'to choose, select,' &c., viii of نخب 'to extract, draw out,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Chosen, selected; choice, select; rare; — a selection; an extract; — an abstract; a compendium; — one of the select; one of the gentry
subs: v.t. To select; to extract; to make an abstract (of, -kā)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian منتخب (muntaxab), taken from Arabic مُنْتَخَب (muntaḵab).

def: s.m. pl. (of muntaḵẖab), Selections; extracts.

ety: act. part. of اِنْتقم 'to take revenge,' &c., viii of نقم, idem
def: part. & s.m. Taking revenge; avenging; — one who punishes, one who takes revenge; — (God) the Punisher, or Avenger.

ety: act. or pass. part. of اِنْتشر 'to be spread, or scattered,' &c., viii of نشر 'to spread,' &c.
def: part. adj. Spread, wide-spread, dispersed; diffused; diffuse; published, propagated, divulged; — explained; — distracted, bewildered

ety: act. part. of اِنْتظم 'to be ordered, or arranged,' &c., viii of نظم 'to join, string; to order, arrange,' &c.
def: part. adj. Ordered, arranged, disposed, adjusted; placed in rows; joined together; strung, threaded (as pearls, &c.); — s.m. (in Pers. & Urdū), One who regulates or places in order; orderer, disposer, arranger; manager, superintendent; an economist.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian منتظم (muntazim), from Arabic مُنْتَظِم (muntaẓim), active participle of اِنْتَظَمَ (intaẓama).

ety: act. part. of اِنْتظر 'to expect; to wait anxiously for,' viii of نظر 'to see, look at, view,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Expecting with impatience, awaiting with anxiety, looking out (for), watching (for), expectant (of); — one who is expecting, or looking out (for), an expectant
subs: v.t. To keep (one) in expectation, to keep (one) waiting
(-kā), To watch (for), look out (for), to expect; to wait (for), to await.

def: s.f. = موٿهہ mūṭh, q.v.
s.m.(?) An astrological term for the conjunction of stars under whose influence one is born.

ety: act. part. of انطق 'to speak articulately,' &c.
def: adj. (in Arith.) Rational (as opp. to 'surd' quantity or number).
adj. (dialec.) Little, small, diminutive; of small quantity, or degree, &c. (syn. laghu; alpa; thoṛā; — cf. munnā, and muniyā, which are prob. fr. the same source as mūnū).

ety: for A. منية, fr. منا (for مني
def: s.f. Wish, desire, hope.

def: part. adj. & s.m. Secluded, solitary; — a solitary man, a recluse, a hermit.

ety: act. part. of اِنْزال 'to be removed; to go or pass away,' &c., viii of زال (for زول
def: part. adj. Passing away, perishing, decaying, failing.

ety: act. part. of اِنْضج 'to cause to ripen, to mature,' &c., iv of نضج 'to ripen,' &c.
def: adj. Suppurative; — digestive.

ety: act. part. of انزل 'to cause to alight,' &c., iv of نزل 'to alight,' &c.
def: part. adj. Causing to alight; receiving hospitably; — emittens semen
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Caused to alight or descend; sent down (from heaven).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَنزِل (manzil), from Arabic مَنْزِل (manzil, “house, dwelling”). First attested in c. 1421 as Old Hindi مَنْزِل (mnzl /⁠manzil⁠/, “abode”).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = mūṛ, or mūṅṛ, qq.v.
s.m. corr. of مول mūl, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑁄𑀭 (mora), from Sanskrit मयूर (mayū́ra).

ety: i.q. munḍ, mūṅḍ, or mūṅṛ
def: s.m. (rustic or dialec.) The head.

ety: prob. S. मुरल+कः
def: s.m. A species of very small fish.
s.m. Name of a Daitya slain by Krishṇa
subs: murārā, murāri, murārī (S. mura+ari), s.m. 'Enemy of Mura,' epithet of Krishṇa or Vishṇu
vulg. murad, s.m. (lit. 'Mura-cutting'), The discus of Krishṇa or Vishṇu (with which he slew Mur).

ety: perf. part. of muṛnā, q.v.
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Turned; twisted; bent, &c.
